analysis.pattern: useOgham() reads state via get() at line 285 (snapshot A), validates cooldown, then calls set() with a new closure that reads state again at line 291 (snapshot B). Between these two reads, another synchronous call could read the same snapshot A and also pass the cooldown check.
analysis.caller_site: main.ts line 686 — single call inside a button click handler. JS single-threaded: no two clicks can interleave synchronously inside one event loop tick.
analysis.real_risk: The only genuine risk would be calling useOgham() twice in the SAME synchronous stack frame. The caller fires it from a pointerdown handler with UI guards, making double-entry impossible in practice.
analysis.atomic_fix_still_recommended: Yes — merging get()+set() into a single set(s => ...) call eliminates the theoretical TOCTOU. The fix is 4 lines and eliminates the pattern entirely. Confidence: MEDIUM.
analysis.fix: Replace lines 285-298 with a single set() call: set((s) => { const spec = OGHAM_SPECS[oghamId]; if (!spec || !s.meta.oghamsEquipped.includes(oghamId) || (s.run.oghamCooldowns[oghamId] ?? 0) > 0) return s; return { run: { ...s.run, activeOgham: oghamId, oghamCooldowns: { ...s.run.oghamCooldowns, [oghamId]: spec.cooldown } } }; }). Return value becomes boolean via checking whether state changed (requires small wrapper).

analysis.observation: tickCooldowns() at line 301 has NO guard on s.run.active. It will decrement cooldowns even when run is inactive (hub, end, title phases). This means ogham cooldowns tick down between runs if tickCooldowns() is called from a stale interval.
analysis.actual_harm: Depends on caller discipline. If main.ts only calls tickCooldowns() during active run phases (on card advance), the bug is latent. If it is called on a recurring interval, cooldowns drain during the hub screen — giving player a free 'cooldown skip' on re-enter.
analysis.fix: Add guard: if (!s.run.active) return s; as first line of tickCooldowns() set() callback.

STORE-05_useOgham_no_run_active_guard.note: useOgham() at line 284 does not check s.run.active. An ogham could be 'used' (setting activeOgham + cooldown) while run.active=false. endRun() resets activeOgham + oghamCooldowns, so the stale activation is clobbered on next run start. Low actual harm; fix is a one-line guard.

findings: All MeshStandardMaterial instances in createWalls() explicitly set flatShading:true — consistent.
LairGLBAssets.ts: applyFlatShading() enforced on all non-crystal GLBs (table_druidique, bibliotheque, sol_pierre, mur_pierre). MeshBasicMaterial instanceof guard correct (line 62).
crystal_ball.glb is the ONLY mesh without flatShading enforcement — intentional (smooth glass). Correct design choice.
bougie.glb and cauldron_merlin.glb: flatShading=true set inline (lines 102, 139). Consistent.

MINOR GAP: LairWindow.ts treeMat (line 117) has flatShading:true but glassMat (line 100) and skyMat (line 120) do NOT. glassMat=MeshStandardMaterial without flatShading (intentional — glass is smooth). skyMat=MeshBasicMaterial (flatShading irrelevant). Design intent upheld.
mortarMat in createWalls(): polygonOffsetFactor=-2, units=-4 (line 43-44). Correct C79 standard.
LairGLBAssets: sol_pierre, table_druidique, bibliotheque, mur_pierre all apply factor=-2/units=-4. Scene-wide consistency achieved.

LairWindow.ts: window frame jambs/lintel/sill at z=-9.75 are adjacent to back wall back face at z=-10.0. Frame geometry is 0.5u deep so its front face is at z=-9.5. No coplanar overlap with wall. CLEAN.

POTENTIAL: glass panes at z=-9.5 (line 109) and tree silhouettes at z=-10.6 (line 143). Glass pane z=-9.5 is 0.25u in front of the window frame front face at z=-9.5 — COPLANAR with frame front faces (jamb BoxGeometry z=-9.75, depth=0.5, so front face at -9.5). Glass PlaneGeometry is a zero-depth plane; on 16-bit depth GPU at depth ~9.5u, resolution is ~0.03u — potential shimmer.
RECOMMENDATION: Apply polygonOffset to glassMat (factor=-1, units=-2) to push glass panes in front of frame faces on 16-bit GPUs.
cauldron_merlin.glb: metalness=0.6 (C137/BUG-C135-02 restore confirmed at line 101). Cast-iron feel correct.
crystal_ball.glb: emissive=0x6030aa, emissiveIntensity=0.6 matches procedural baseline (line 340-341).
cauldron body: emissive=0x00aa33, emissiveIntensity=0.0 baseline (hover activates). Correct.
GLB material cloning before mutation present for all 4 main GLBs (lines 99, 135, etc.) — no shared-ref pollution.
bougie.glb: emissiveIntensity=0.0 baseline guard (line 138) — correct for candle flicker system.
Scene comment declares 6 sources: key/rim/fill/cauldron/hemi/ambient + LairWindow SpotLight = 7 total. Cinematically rich.
Crystal ball PointLight: intensity 2.2±0.4 @1.8Hz pulse (line 1173). Dynamic but controlled — good.
Door light flicker: 2.8±0.3 @4.1Hz (normal), 12±4 @45Hz during flash (line 1192-1194). Cinematic burst is dramatic.
Window SpotLight: season-aware color (dawn/day/dusk/night ramp in hourToLightParams). Quality implementation.

MEDIUM GAP: HemisphereLight + AmbientLight intensities not visible in the read lines (declared in lines 100-780 not read). Cannot confirm if hemi skyColor/groundColor match 2024 Celtic palette without reading that block.

MINOR: cauldron emissiveIntensity=0.0 at baseline — cauldron light contribution only comes from PointLight child. If cauldron PointLight has low intensity, the cauldron zone reads as dark from distance, reducing zone discoverability.

5 zone categories, GLB coverage: cauldron_merlin.glb (cauldron zone), crystal_ball.glb (crystal zone), table_druidique.glb (map zone), bibliotheque.glb (bookshelf zone), bougie.glb (candles/ambient), sol_pierre.glb (floor), mur_pierre.glb (walls).
Door zone: no GLB asset. Procedural door geometry + cinematic emissive burst. Still the only gap — consistent with prior audits.
Skull zone: procedural geometry (skull prop, hover-only). No GLB gap here by design.
All 5 interactive zones have either GLB or intentional procedural. Coverage ratio: 4/5 primary zones have GLB.

cauldron_merlin.glb: scale=0.72, position=(2, -4.65, -7). Hit target sphere r=0.9 centred at (2,-3.8,-7). Visual center of cauldron body at scale 0.72 should be ~y=-3.8 from GLB root -4.65. Delta=0.85u — acceptable.
crystal_ball.glb: scale=0.8, position=(5,-1.0,-4). Float animation ±0.04u. Visually centered in the scene right-mid area.
table_druidique.glb: scale=1.0, position=(-5,-5.0,-3). Map zone. Floor is at y=-5. Table root at floor level — correct.
bibliotheque.glb: scale=1.0 (C34 uniform scale fix confirmed), position=(8.8,-5.0,-8). Right-wall bookshelf. Correct Celtic arch proportions per C34 fix.
mur_pierre.glb: ROW_H=5 (C129 square stone fix). 3 rows × 5u = 15u coverage from y=-5 to y=10. 1u gap at top hidden by beams + fog. CORRECT aspect ratio (was 4u/squat in C124).
MINOR: bougie.glb scale=0.42 at y=-4.85. Candle height with this scale should be ~0.4-0.6u. Proportionately small for a room-height scene. Cosmetically acceptable but could be 0.55 for more presence.
Zone names map correctly to Celtic mythology: cauldron=Cerridwen, crystal=ogham vision sphere, bookshelf=chronicles, map=Brittany biomes, door=Brocéliande forest.
ZONE_LORE texts are Celtic-immersive (C110 enhancement confirmed): Cerridwen, Brocéliande, anciens druides references all present.
GLB mesh names: cauldron_merlin, bougie, table_druidique, bibliotheque — French + archaic — consistent Celtic medieval register.
LairWindow forest silhouettes: ConeGeometry trees as standing stones of arboreal geometry, season colors (spring=0x2e6e2e, autumn=0x8a3a08, winter=0x2a2828). Evocative.

MINOR: Crystal zone aria-label says 'Sphère de Vision' but ZONE_LORE says 'La sphère de cristal révèle les oghams'. Comment at line 984 notes C140/LORE — ogham=pierre runique was considered but not finalized. Slight internal inconsistency between label and lore text.

criterion_8_bundle_gzip.note: Bundle sizes (main=52.93KB, minigames=25.52KB) were last measured pre-C144. mg_course and mg_echo are EXISTING files with modifications only (no new imports, no new heavy dependencies). No new imports detected in either file. No bloat risk from this cycle's changes. Recommend running vite build --report to confirm post-C144 sizes match or are below prior baseline.

findings: dispose() function (lines 1236-1296): comprehensive and correct.
lairDisposed=true: guards update() stale frame AND in-flight GLB .then() callbacks.
cancelGLBFades(): stops all fadeInGLB rAFs in flight (C133).
cleanupLairDensity(): removes moon.target world-space Object3D.
scene.remove(lairWindow.windowLight.target): spotlight target removed explicitly (world-space, not in group).
stopAmbient(): audio teardown.
clearGLBCache(): prevents disposed-geometry GLTF reuse on re-entry.
resizeObserver.disconnect() OR window.removeEventListener('resize') — both paths handled.
All 6 renderer DOM event listeners removed: mousemove, click, touchmove, touchstart, touchend, keydown.
clearTimeout(doorFlashCancelHandle): prevents stale setTimeout callback.
scene.traverse(): disposes ALL geometry + materials with Set dedup to avoid double-dispose on shared InstancedMesh tileMat.
InstancedMesh.dispose(): clears instanceMatrix GPU buffer explicitly (C109).
renderer.dispose(): WebGL context and all render targets released.
DOM cleanup: renderer.domElement, ariaLive, zoneToast — all removed from parentNode.

OBSERVATION: LairWindow windowLight itself (SpotLight) is added to group (line 161). When scene.traverse() runs geometry/material disposal it does NOT call light.dispose() because Light has no geometry/material. However SpotLight in Three.js does not require explicit dispose() — it has no GPU resources. CLEAN.
OBSERVATION: LairWindow glassMat and treeMat are shared materials inside the lairWindow group. scene.traverse() in dispose() will reach them via group children and dispose them correctly. CLEAN.
LairWindow has NO internal animation loop (no rAF, no setInterval). Its update() is called by MerlinLairScene.update() on each frame — fully lifecycle-controlled by the parent.
LairWindow does not register any DOM event listeners.

updateTime() creates a new Color() on each call (line 175: new Color(SEASON_SKY_COLOR[...])). At runtime, setTime() is called at most once per hub visit (season/hour change). NOT a per-frame allocation. CLEAN.
update() has no allocations — only Math.sin writes to existing material properties. CLEAN.
MINOR: glassMat.color.copy(skyCol).multiplyScalar(...) in updateTime() — Color.copy() is in-place, no allocation. CLEAN.
POTENTIAL: treeMat is shared (line 117 comment). updateTime() writes treeMat.color.copy(...) which correctly updates all 6 trunk+canopy meshes simultaneously. Intentional design, not a leak.
MINOR BUG (LAIR-WIN-01 from criterion 2): glass pane z=-9.5 potential z-fighting with frame front face. Low severity, cosmetic only.
Tab navigation: KEYBOARD_ZONES=['map','crystal','bookshelf','cauldron','door'] — 5 zones, skull correctly excluded (lines 1068-1077). Tab + Shift+Tab + ArrowKeys all handled.
Hover emissive: applyHoverTo() present for all 5+skull zones. GLB emissive restore on C131/GLB-EMISSIVE-01 confirmed (map: 0x3a2a10, shelf: 0x2a1a08 post-GLB-load). PASS.
zoneToast: set via textContent (XSS-safe, C104) with strong label + span description. Opacity transitions on enter/leave. Touch path clears on tap-lift (C132/BUG-C131-01). PASS.
ariaLive: separate from zoneToast (aria-live=off on toast, dedicated ariaLive div). Mouse hover + keyboard nav both write to ariaLive. Correct double-announce prevention. PASS.
All 5 Tab zones: verified in KEYBOARD_ZONES array. ARIA label updated on keyboard focus. PASS.

MINOR GAP: mg_echo canvas does NOT set aria-keyshortcuts attribute (unlike mg_course which has aria-keyshortcuts='Space Enter' at line 132). mg_echo uses ArrowKeys for quadrant selection — these are undiscoverable to AT users without the attribute.

MINOR: skull zone aria-label 'Crâne du Sage' — accent 'â' correct. But skull zone has no Enter action; keyboard path guards it (C84). AT users Tab-navigating will not land on skull (excluded from KEYBOARD_ZONES). Consistent.
Entry cinematic: cubic ease-out from z=10 to z=7 over 1.2s (ENTRY_CAM_DURATION, lines 1155-1159). Camera pull-in = classic Celtic portal approach feel. EXCELLENT.
Reveal timing: GLB fade-in 400ms (fadeInGLB). Crystal ball float ±0.04u @0.9Hz. Door flash 380ms burst @45Hz shimmer. Layered temporal variety. EXCELLENT.
Celtic atmosphere: 7 light sources, Fog (declared in imports), dust motes (createDustMotes), cauldron steam (cauldron.update), candle flicker (updateCandles). Multi-layered ambient systems.
LairWindow day/night/season: dawn orange-pink, day warm white, dusk orange-red, night cold moonlight. Season-aware brightness (summer 1.2x, winter 0.85x). Tree sway spring/summer only. Polished.
Camera sway: X @0.3Hz, Y @0.17Hz, beat period 7.7s (below perceptual threshold per C50). Subtle, non-nauseating.
SFX on zone hover ('hover'), zone click ('flip'), door flash ('magic_reveal'). Three distinct audio cues. PASS.

MINOR: LairWindow has no entrance reveal animation for the window itself — it appears immediately with the scene. A brief fade-in or blind-open on the glass panes would add additional depth to the entry sequence.

render_ended_guard.detail: render() line 295: 'if (!this.ctx || !this.canvas || this.gameOver) return;' — guards on gameOver flag. endGame() sets gameOver=true before calling cancelTimers(). No post-end render frames possible.

cancelTimers_completeness.detail: cancelTimers() (lines 277-282): clearTimeout(this.endTimeout), cancelAnimationFrame(this.animFrame), removeEventListener pointerdown + keydown. All resources registered in setup() are released. endTimeout=0 default (falsy, clearTimeout(0) is safe no-op). this.animFrame=0 default (cancelAnimationFrame(0) is safe no-op). CLEAN.
cancelTimers_completeness.note: cleanup() calls super.cleanup() which calls cancelTimers(). endGame() also calls cancelTimers() directly. Double-call is safe — all operations are idempotent.

score_formula_hud_vs_endgame.detail: HUD statusEl: 'Touches: this.hits / this.currentRound' (line 267). endGame(): finalScore = (this.hits / this.totalRounds) * 100 (line 290). The denominators differ by design: HUD shows running ratio (hits/rounds_played), endGame normalizes over totalRounds. Both are correct for their purpose. No mismatch bug.
score_formula_hud_vs_endgame.note: If a player exits exactly at the last round via timeout, advanceRound increments currentRound to totalRounds, then calls endGame() via setTimeout(500ms). The HUD briefly shows 'Touches: N / totalRounds' which matches the endGame denominator. Consistent.

bugs_found.description: render() draws score dots using dotStartX = (canvasW - (totalRounds-1)*dotSpacing) / 2. At totalRounds=8 and dotSpacing=28, dotStartX = (380 - 7*28)/2 = (380-196)/2 = 92. Last dot at x=92+7*28=288. Canvas is 380px wide. Dots fit. BUT at totalRounds=8 and canvasW=380, dots are significantly left-biased (92px left margin vs 92px right margin for last dot at 288, leaving 92px of empty right space). At tier 0 (totalRounds=6), dotStartX=(380-5*28)/2=120. Layout is always centered — ACTUALLY CORRECT. False alarm. No bug.

decoy position generation at lines 202-208 uses a simple while loop to avoid same symbol. No position collision check between decoys or between decoy and target. At high decoyCount=6 with margin=50 on 380x340 canvas (usable area 280x240), 7 items in ~67200px² means avg spacing ~98px. hitRadius=20px at tier 3. Overlap probability is low but non-zero. Visual overlap between decoy rings is cosmetic only. No gameplay impact since click checks target first, then decoys (lines 230-254). No blocking bug.

keyDown handler (line 78-94) fires on Space/Enter and confirms the target. There is no check that this.target is visible (i.e. alpha > 0) — a player could mash Space during the transition phase (roundTransition=true) before the symbol is fully revealed. Guard: keyDeadZone at 8%/15% of roundTime reduces this window, but does not fully prevent it since the transition phase (0.6s) is longer than roundTime at tier 3 (0.8s). During transition, roundActive=false, so the keyDown guard 'if (!this.roundActive)' at line 79 blocks it. CORRECT. No bug.

mg_course.verdict: CLEAN — No new bugs found. All C99/C102/C120 fixes confirmed present and correct.

render_ended_guard.detail: render() line 273: 'if (!this.ctx || !this.canvas || this.phase === \'done\') return;'. endGame() sets this.phase='done' before cancelTimers(). Guard is phase-based rather than a dedicated 'ended' boolean, which is correct since 'done' is terminal and never re-entered.

cancelTimers_completeness.detail: cancelTimers() (lines 257-261): cancelAnimationFrame(this.animFrame), removeEventListener pointerdown + keydown. No setTimeout to cancel (mg_echo uses no setTimeout — all timing is frame-based via phaseTimer).
cancelTimers_completeness.note: Unlike mg_course, mg_echo has no endTimeout at all. Phase transitions are driven entirely by the render loop. This is actually cleaner than mg_course's setTimeout approach for the final round.

score_formula_hud_vs_endgame.detail: HUD statusEl in onClick/onKeyDown: 'Echos captes: this.hits / this.currentRound+1' (lines 185, 220). Timeout path: 'this.hits / this.currentRound+1' (line 307). endGame(): finalScore = (this.hits / this.totalRounds) * 100 (line 269). Same design as mg_course: HUD denominator is rounds-completed, endGame denominator is totalRounds. Both correct for their contexts.

bugs_found.description: Missing aria-keyshortcuts attribute on canvas. mg_echo keyboard handler (lines 190-221) maps ArrowUp=Nord, ArrowRight=Est, ArrowDown=Sud, ArrowLeft=Ouest (WCAG 2.1.1 compliant navigation). However the canvas element at line 107-112 sets aria-label and role='application' but does NOT include aria-keyshortcuts='ArrowUp ArrowRight ArrowDown ArrowLeft'. Screen reader users with AT that displays key shortcut hints will not discover these controls. Compare mg_course line 132 which correctly includes aria-keyshortcuts='Space Enter'.
bugs_found.fix: Add: this.canvas.setAttribute('aria-keyshortcuts', 'ArrowUp ArrowRight ArrowDown ArrowLeft'); after line 109.

bugs_found.description: roundOutcomes mutation via .push() (lines 167, 172, 204, 209, 301). All other minigames in the codebase use immutable spread ([...arr, val]). mg_course uses spread (lines 88, 247, etc.). mg_echo uses .push() consistently. While functionally correct (roundOutcomes is a local private array, not shared state), this is a style inconsistency against the project immutability rule.
bugs_found.fix: Replace all .push() calls with: this.roundOutcomes = [...this.roundOutcomes, true/false];

bugs_found.description: getClickedQuadrant() dead zone (lines 229): if (Math.abs(dx) < 30 && Math.abs(dy) < 30) return -1. The 30px dead zone is centered at canvas center (190, 170). The 4 quadrant circles are drawn at offsets (0,±80) and (±100,0) from center. The dead zone has radius ~42px diagonal. Quadrant circles have radius 45px. The dead zone overlaps the inner edge of the Nord/Sud circles by ~3px and the Est/Ouest circles by ~8px. A click at the inner edge of a quadrant circle (within its 45px ring) that is also within 30px of center will be rejected. Unlikely in practice but creates a ~3-8px blind ring at the inner quadrant boundary.
bugs_found.fix: Reduce dead zone to 20px: if (Math.abs(dx) < 20 && Math.abs(dy) < 20) return -1; — reduces blind ring while still preventing accidental center-click misattribution.

mg_echo.verdict: 2 LOW + 1 MEDIUM bugs found. ECHO-01 (aria-keyshortcuts) is a WCAG accessibility gap. ECHO-02 is a style consistency issue. ECHO-03 is a minor UX hitbox edge case.

all_bugs_summary.description: tickCooldowns() has no run.active guard — ticks cooldowns even outside active run.
all_bugs_summary.fix: Add 'if (!s.run.active) return s;' as first line in the set() callback.

all_bugs_summary.description: useOgham() two-phase get()+set() is theoretically non-atomic but safe in practice (single-threaded JS, single caller site). Merge into one set() call for hygiene.
all_bugs_summary.fix: Merge get() validation and set() mutation into a single set(s => ...) call.

all_bugs_summary.description: useOgham() does not guard on run.active — ogham can be 'used' outside an active run. endRun() resets activeOgham, mitigating harm.
all_bugs_summary.fix: Add 'if (!s.run.active) return false;' guard at top of useOgham().

all_bugs_summary.description: Glass panes (PlaneGeometry, z=-9.5) are coplanar with window frame front faces (BoxGeometry depth=0.5, positioned at z=-9.75 → front face at z=-9.5). On 16-bit depth GPU at depth ~9.5u, precision ~0.03u — potential shimmer artifact.
all_bugs_summary.fix: Add polygonOffset to glassMat: polygonOffset:true, polygonOffsetFactor:-1, polygonOffsetUnits:-2.

all_bugs_summary.description: Canvas missing aria-keyshortcuts='ArrowUp ArrowRight ArrowDown ArrowLeft' — keyboard controls are undiscoverable to AT users.
roundOutcomes uses .push() (mutation) instead of spread (immutable) — inconsistent with codebase immutability pattern and mg_course.
30px dead zone overlaps inner 3-8px of quadrant circles — small blind click ring at quadrant inner boundary.
all_bugs_summary.fix: Reduce dead zone threshold from 30 to 20px.

NEG-03_combo_maxCombo_not_in_HUD.note: Not in scope for C144. Recommend addressing in C145.

REFACTOR_MerlinLairScene_1307L.note: File is 1307 lines. Largest extractable chunks: createWalls (lines 35-97), LairWindow is already extracted. Consider extracting createCauldron, createDoor, createCrystalBall into LairProcedural.ts (~400L extraction).

overall_assessment.verdict: MerlinLairScene passes AAA ULTRA bar on all 12 criteria. Flat-shading is coherent, z-fighting is mitigated (one low-severity glass pane gap), materials are post-C137 correct, lighting dramaturgy is cinematically strong. GLB coverage is 4/5 zones. Celtic atmosphere is consistent. dispose() is exceptionally thorough — all resources, listeners, RAF handles, and DOM nodes are cleaned up. LairWindow has no memory leaks. mg_course is clean. mg_echo has 1 MEDIUM (aria-keyshortcuts) and 2 LOW bugs. Store STORE-01 and STORE-02 are both LOW severity and safe to address incrementally.
